en: Then Job answered the LORD and said:
"I know that you can do all things, and that no purpose of yours can be thwarted.
zh: “我知道你萬事都能作， 你的旨意是不能攔阻的。

en: 'Who is this that hides counsel without knowledge?' Therefore I have uttered what I did not understand, things too wonderful for me, which I did not know.
zh: 這以無知無識的言語使 神的旨意模糊不清的是誰呢？ 所以我說了我所不明白的； 這些事太奇妙，是我不曉得的。

en: 'Hear, and I will speak; I will question you, and you make it known to me.'
zh: 求你聽我，我要說話； 我要問你，你要告訴我。

en: I had heard of you by the hearing of the ear, but now my eye sees you;
zh: 我從前只是風聞有你， 但現在親眼看見你。

en: therefore I despise myself, and repent in dust and ashes."
zh: 因此我厭惡自己， 在塵土和灰燼中懊悔。”

en: After the LORD had spoken these words to Job, the LORD said to Eliphaz the Temanite: "My anger burns against you and against your two friends, for you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has.
zh: 耶和華對約伯說了這些話以後，就對提幔人以利法說：“我要向你和你的兩個朋友生氣，因為你們講論我，不如我的僕人約伯說的對。

en: Now therefore take seven bulls and seven rams and go to my servant Job and offer up a burnt offering for yourselves. And my servant Job shall pray for you, for I will accept his prayer not to deal with you according to your folly. For you have not spoken of me what is right, as my servant Job has."
zh: 現在你們要為自己取七頭公牛、七隻公羊，到我的僕人約伯那裡去，為你們獻上燔祭，我的僕人約伯就替你們禱告；我悅納他的禱告，就不按照你們的愚昧待你們。你們講論我，不如我的僕人約伯說的對。”

en: So Eliphaz the Temanite and Bildad the Shuhite and Zophar the Naamathite went and did what the LORD had told them, and the LORD accepted Job's prayer.
zh: 於是提幔人以利法、書亞人比勒達、拿瑪人瑣法，照著耶和華吩咐他們的去行，耶和華就悅納約伯的禱告。

en: And the LORD restored the fortunes of Job, when he had prayed for his friends. And the LORD gave Job twice as much as he had before.
zh: 約伯替他的朋友禱告，耶和華就恢復約伯原來的景況，還照約伯以前所有的一切加倍賜給他。

en: Then came to him all his brothers and sisters and all who had known him before, and ate bread with him in his house. And they showed him sympathy and comforted him for all the evil that the LORD had brought upon him. And each of them gave him a piece of money and a ring of gold.
zh: 約伯的眾兄弟姊妹和以前認識的人都來見他，在他家裡與他一同吃飯，又因耶和華降予他的一切災禍都對他表同情，安慰他，每人送他一塊銀子和一個金環。

en: And the LORD blessed the latter days of Job more than his beginning. And he had 14,000 sheep, 6,000 camels, 1,000 yoke of oxen, and 1,000 female donkeys.
zh: 這樣，耶和華後來賜給約伯的福比先前更多：他有一萬四千隻羊、六千匹駱駝、一千對牛、一千頭母驢。

en: He had also seven sons and three daughters.
zh: 他也有七個兒子和三個女兒。

en: And he called the name of the first daughter Jemimah, and the name of the second Keziah, and the name of the third Keren-happuch.
zh: 他給長女起名叫耶米瑪，次女名叫基洗亞，三女名叫基連．哈樸。

en: And in all the land there were no women so beautiful as Job's daughters. And their father gave them an inheritance among their brothers.
zh: 在那全地中，找不到像約伯女兒那麼美貌的婦女。她們的父親在她們兄弟中間把產業分給她們。

en: And after this Job lived 140 years, and saw his sons, and his sons' sons, four generations.
zh: 此後，約伯又活了一百四十年，得見他的兒孫到四代。

en: And Job died, an old man, and full of days.
zh: 這樣，約伯年紀老邁，壽終而死。
